1307.report:
On the right side in the thalamus-pineal region, an irregular mass lesion 
measuring approximately 1.9 cm x 2.1 cm x 2.1 cm is located with hypointense signal 
in T1 and hyperintense signal in T2. After contrast administration, there is mild 
heterogeneous ring enhancement. There is dilation of the third ventricle and bilateral 
lateral ventricles. No abnormal signal areas are seen within the remaining brain 
parenchyma, and there are no abnormal enhancing lesions after contrast. The gray-
white matter differentiation is clear, and the sulci, fissures, cisterns, and ventricles 
appear normal in size and shape. There is no midline shift. The morphology and 
signal of the skull and scalp tissues are normal.

2617.report: Two patchy lesions are located in the right basal ganglia and right temporal lobe, with isointense to slightly hypointense signal in T1, slightly hypointense to hyperintense signal in T2. FLAIR shows hyperintense signal. There is a patchy edema band surrounding the lesions. After contrast, there is prominent heterogeneous ring enhancement. The bilateral lateral ventricles are compressed, with a localized left shift of the midline structures. The ventricular system shows no significant dilation, and there is no obvious widening of the sulci or cisterns.
2617.location: Right basal ganglia and right temporal lobe.

Multiple patchy lesions are located in the bilateral thalami, with slightly hypointense signals in T1, slightly hyperintense signals in T2, high signal intensity on FLAIR, and no enhancement after contrast. Multiple similar signal lesions are located in the bilateral frontal lobes and right insular cortex. The gray-white matter boundaries are clear, and the sizes and shapes of sulci, fissures, cisterns, and ventricles are normal, with no midline structure shift.

2227.report:
On the right cerebral peduncle, right internal capsule, bilateral frontal and 
temporal lobes, right parietal lobe, and corpus callosum, there are multiple scattered 
foci showing hypointense T1 and hyperintense T2 signals, with hyperintense FLAIR 
signals. A slightly hyperintense T1 focus with a peripheral hypointense T2 signal, 
located on the right body of the corpus callosum, shows ring enhancement after 
contrast, while other lesions show no significant enhancement after contrast. The 
remaining brain parenchyma shows no abnormal signal foci or enhancement lesions, 
with clear gray-white matter boundaries. The ventricular system appears normal in 
size and morphology, with no midline structure shift, and normal sulci.

1597.report:
The 59mm x 48mm x 36mm lesion is located in the left cerebellar hemisphere, 
isointense to hypointense in T1, hyperintense in T2, with peripheral hyperintense and 
central isointense in FLAIR. The lesion has a clear boundary and shows significant 
enhancement of the cyst wall after contrast administration. The cyst wall is smooth 
but uneven in thickness, exerting slight pressure on the adjacent fourth ventricle. 
There is no apparent dilation of the supratentorial ventricular system, and the sulci, 
fissures, and cisterns appear normal. There is no midline shift.

1415.report: In the left frontal lobe, a nodular lesion is noted, slightly hypointense in T1 and hyperintense in T2. The right temporal region contains a nodular lesion, isointense in both T1 and T2. The right parietal lobe shows a patchy lesion, hypointense in T1 and hyperintense in T2, with FLAIR demonstrating hypointense internally and hyperintense externally. Bilaterally in the frontal and parietal lobes, multiple patchy lesions are hypointense in T1 and hyperintense in T2. Multiple cap-like and linear lesions are present in both lateral ventricle anterior and posterior horns and periventricular areas, also hypointense in T1 and hyperintense in T2. Both cerebellar hemispheres exhibit multiple small patchy lesions, hypointense in T1 and hyperintense in T2. FLAIR reveals slightly hyperintense in the left frontal lobe, equal signal in the right temporal region, slightly higher signal in the right parietal lobe, high signal bilaterally in the frontal and parietal lobes, high signal in both lateral ventricle anterior and posterior horns and periventricular areas, and low signal in both cerebellar hemispheres. No significant enhancement after contrast is observed in the bilateral frontal and parietal lobes, with mild uniform enhancement in the right temporal region and mild to moderate progressive enhancement in the left frontal lobe. The gray-white matter boundaries are clear, the ventricular system is normal in size and shape, there is no midline structure shift, and the sulci appear normal.

1628.report:
A 15mm × 11mm nodular lesion at the upper part of the fourth ventricle, 
predominantly isointense on T1 and T2, with patchy hypointense regions on T1 and 
hyperintense regions on T2. After contrast administration, mild patchy enhancement 
is observed. Obstruction at the upper part of the fourth ventricle, with significant 
dilatation of the cerebral aqueduct, third ventricle, and bilateral lateral ventricles. 
Adjacent to the bilateral lateral ventricles, linear lesions slightly hyperintense on T2 
and hypointense on T1, showing hyperintense signal on FLAIR sequence. No midline 
shift.

2793.report:
An 8.3 cm x 7.2 cm irregular cystic-solid lesion, measuring approximately 8.3 cm 
x 7.2 cm in size, is located in the left cerebellar hemisphere, with hypointense signal 
in T1, isointense to hyperintense signal in T2. A 5.8 cm x 5.2 cm clear boundary 
mass lesion, measuring approximately 5.8 cm x 5.2 cm in size, is located within it, 
with relatively clear boundaries. The lesion protrudes into the cervical spinal canal. 
After contrast administration, the edges of the lesion and the internal mass show 
slightly heterogeneous enhancement. The lesion compresses and displaces the 
adjacent right cerebellar hemisphere, brainstem, and spinal cord to the right. There is 
marked dilatation of the fourth ventricle and supratentorial ventricles, with a slight 
rightward shift of midline structures.
